facts.claim: Cadastros-UI cluster manages master-data CRUD for 4 core entities: Empresa (organization), Estabelecimento (facility), Leito (bed), and Usuário (professional/user); each has a React form component with conditional field rendering, permission-gated access, and normalization pipelines.

Field editability is constrained by company tipo (manual vs automatica): nome and código fields are always disabled for automatica companies (external-system-synced records), regardless of form mode or admin override; manual companies may edit these fields in modal (creation) mode only.

Permission-gated visibility gates credentials and CRUD actions: can_manage_camera hides camera login/senha/IP fields; can_manage_usuario gates setores field on user submit and enables professional delete action; can_manage_grupo (implied from FormEditGrupo) gates group member editing.

Distribution of 20 extracted rules: validation (10), workflow (4), eligibility (3), decision-tree (2), formula (1), threshold (1). Status: OK (16), AMBIGUOUS (3, flagged for verification), DISCREPANCY (1). Verification verdict: NOT_APPLICABLE (19), DISCREPANCY (1, CEP mask format).

Brazilian identifiers and formats are preserved and enforced across forms: CPF (11 digits, mask 000.000.000-00, unformatted on submit), CNPJ (14 digits, 00.000.000/0000-00), CEP (8 digits, 00.000-000 — but catalog shows DISCREPANCY: official Correios format is NNNNN-NNN with single hyphen, no period), phone (10-11 digits, length-dependent mask), BRL currency (Intl pt-BR format), byte sizes (1024 base with SI labels).

Form mode (modal vs in_page) gates required-field enforcement: in FormUsuario, nome/cpf/username/password/email rules are applied only in modal (creation) mode; in_page (edit-in-place) mode renders same fields without required rule. Duplicate logic in FormEstabelecimento/FormLeito/FormSetor: they gate field VISIBILITY/DISABLED by mode but enforce required-ness UNCONDITIONALLY (Phase-1 note corrected in catalog).

constraints.text: AMBIGUOUS (RULE-CADASTROS-UI-005): new user password field is auto-filled with unformatted CPF digits on CPF input change, using CPF (semi-public identifier) as default. Requires verification: is this a convenience feature (overridable) or a security gap (CPF as initial credential)?

AMBIGUOUS (RULE-CADASTROS-UI-006): signature PIN field initialized to hardcoded literal 'Homecare@Vidaconecta' for all users, rendered disabled (non-editable). Requires verification: is this a placeholder (overridden server-side) or an actual shared credential submission?

DISCREPANCY (RULE-CADASTROS-UI-016): CEP (Brazilian postal code) is formatted as 00.000-000 (regex with period + hyphen); official Correios format is NNNNN-NNN (5-3 with single hyphen, explicitly states 'não deverão ser sublinhados ou separados por ponto'). Mask rounds-trip with unformat; requires correction in v2.
constraints.source_ref: cadastros-ui.yaml RULE-CADASTROS-UI-016, verify:true, verdict:DISCREPANCY, reference:'Correios postal format'

constraints.text: AMBIGUOUS (RULE-CADASTROS-UI-001): FilterLeitos passes literal string 'unknown' to onFilter callback (unlike FilterOcupacoes which converts 'todos' to undefined). Requires verification: does backend special-case 'unknown' string as no-filter, or is default occupancy filter silently broken?

Duplicated logic (not shared code): Form{Estabelecimento,Leito}.tsx independently implement identical disabled=(disableAll || tipo !== 'manual') predicate for nome/código fields (lines 39-60 vs 40-56, commit f9656be). Cleanup candidate for v2.

RULE-CADASTROS-UI-019: image-file extension check is case-SENSITIVE (PNG/JPG uppercase not matched); likely latent bug since modern file systems and user uploads often preserve original case. Confidence: medium.
constraints.source_ref: cadastros-ui.yaml RULE-CADASTROS-UI-019, status:OK, notes:'likely latent bug'

systemic_issues.description: User credential defaults (CPF as password, hardcoded PIN) are ambiguous security gaps; verify backend behavior and establish explicit policy on initialization vs placeholder vs submission.
FilterLeitos tri-state enum handling (literal 'unknown' string vs undefined sentinel) inconsistent with FilterOcupacoes; verify backend contract.
CEP mask format (00.000-000 with period) violates official Correios standard (NNNNN-NNN with single hyphen). Requires correction in v2.
Duplicated form-field disabled logic in Form{Estabelecimento,Leito}.tsx (independent implementation, not shared); consolidate to shared utility.
Image extension check is case-sensitive (PNG/JPG uppercase not matched); likely latent bug on mixed-case user-supplied filenames.

pt_br_vocabularies.uf_codes: 27 IBGE codes (AC, AL, AP, AM, BA, CE, DF, ES, GO, MA, MT, MS, MG, PA, PB, PR, PE, PI, RJ, RN, RS, RO, RR, SC, SP, SE, TO)

load_bearing_rules.brief: Leito/Estabelecimento nome and código locked for automatica companies (external-system-synced) regardless of form mode or admin override
CPF field auto-fills password with unformatted CPF digits for new users (AMBIGUOUS: security gap or convenience?)
Hardcoded 'Homecare@Vidaconecta' signature PIN for all users, disabled/non-editable (AMBIGUOUS: placeholder or actual credential?)
Company tipo field only rendered in modal (creation) mode; not editable after creation. Cor_primaria hex color round-trip strips/re-adds '#' for HTML input type=color
FormUsuario submit normalizes nome/username (trim/lowercase), extracts foto_perfil.b64, unformats CPF, gates setores to can_manage_usuario permission
Required-field validation gates on form mode: FormUsuario applies rules only in modal; FormEstabelecimento/Leito/Setor apply unconditionally. Correction note in Phase-1.
Brazilian identifier masks (CPF/CNPJ/CEP/phone/BRL/bytes) with round-trip unformat. CEP format DISCREPANCY: 00.000-000 (period) vs official Correios NNNNN-NNN (hyphen only)
Shared antd-style required-field rule: {required:true, message:'Este campo é obrigatório!'} applied across all forms
Email format validation: emailPattern regex allows most special chars in local part, domain labels 1-63 chars, TLD optional, anchored match
Camera credential fields (login_camera, senha_camera, ip_camera) visibility gated on can_manage_camera permission

open_questions: Does FilterLeitos backend special-case the literal 'unknown' string as no-filter, or is this a latent bug?
Are RULE-CADASTROS-UI-005 and RULE-CADASTROS-UI-006 security gaps or intentional placeholders overridden server-side?
Should CEP mask be corrected to official Correios format (NNNNN-NNN, hyphen only) in v2?
Is FormUsuario the only form that actually gates required-ness by mode, or is Phase-1 correction complete?
